data.title: AngloGold Ashanti reported record $2.9bn free cash flow in 2025 with $875m interim dividend for Q4, totaling $1.8bn for the year.
data.description: In 2025, AngloGold Ashanti plc reported a record free cash flow of $2.9 billion due to robust production growth, cost control, and an increased average gold price per ounce. The company declared an interim dividend of $875 million for Q4 2025, bringing the total payout for the year to $1.8 billion, the largest ever. AngloGold Ashanti met its targets for gold production and sustaining capital expenditure for the year, showcasing its strong performance.

data.title: Anglogold Ashanti Q4 earnings missed estimates by -2.06%, with EPS of $1.9 vs $1.94. Revenue up $1.31B YoY. [Benzinga]
data.description: Anglogold Ashanti (NYSE: AU) revealed its Q4 earnings on Friday, February 20, 2026 at 06:00 AM. The earnings report indicated that the company fell short of estimated earnings by -2.06%, with an EPS of $1.9 compared to the predicted $1.94. Revenue witnessed a significant increase of $1.31 billion from the previous year. For further details on the company's financial performance, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Hudbay Minerals Q4 earnings missed estimates by -43.59%, EPS $0.22 vs. $0.39. Revenue up $148 million from last year.
data.description: Hudbay Minerals (NYSE: HBM) recently announced its Q4 earnings on February 20, 2026, at 06:00 AM. The company fell short of estimated earnings by -43.59%, with reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.22 compared to the estimated $0.39. Revenue saw an increase of $148.00 million compared to the previous year. Analyzing past earnings, the company failed to meet EPS expectations. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Coinbase Institutional's Brett Tejpaul sees momentum in tokenization and bank partnerships despite Bitcoin's drop. Top 20 banks now rely on Coinbase; 90% US ETF volume captured. Bank integrations accelerated after GENIUS Act for stablecoin clarity. Bank Achieved partnerships with 5 top-20 global banks aiming for all 20.
data.description: During the World Economic Forum in Davos, Brett Tejpaul, Co-CEO of Coinbase Institutional, expressed his optimism towards Bitcoin, citing its fixed supply and increasing demand as key drivers. Despite Bitcoin's recent price fluctuations around $70,000 following a February correction, Tejpaul remains positive about its long-term outlook. Tejpaul highlighted the growing momentum in bank partnerships and tokenization in the industry. He mentioned that five out of the world's top 20 banks now rely on Coinbase's infrastructure. Tejpaul's extensive traditional finance (TradFi) background at Barclays and JP Morgan has prepared him for the institutional adoption of cryptocurrencies. Tejpaul co-founded Coinbase's institutional business, aiming to combine traditional finance rigor with blockchain innovation. With the recent passage of the GENIUS Act providing stablecoin clarity, Coinbase's bank integrations have accelerated post-Davos. Tejpaul aims for partnerships with all of the world's top 20 banks to further drive mass adoption of cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Key press releases this week: eBay buys Depop, Ski-Doo launches new features, Keurig Dr Pepper unveils beverage line-up, updates from Toy Fair and KBIS events.
data.description: In recent news, eBay made a significant move by acquiring Depop. Ski-Doo also unveiled some new features, while Keurig Dr Pepper introduced its beverage lineup for the year. Additionally, the notable highlights from the 2026 Toy Fair and KBIS events were summarized. These updates came from New York on February 20, 2026. The acquisition of Depop by eBay, the latest features from Ski-Doo, the release of Keurig Dr Pepper's beverage lineup, and the major headlines from the 2026 Toy Fair and KBIS events were emphasized.

data.title: Axelspace partners with Tri-Sat and Mitsui Bussan for Japan MoD satellite constellation project.
data.description: In Tokyo, Axelspace Corporation, a renowned developer and operator of microsatellites with a vision of "Space within Your Reach," has revealed its partnership details for the Ministry of Defense's satellite constellation initiative. In a recent announcement, Axelspace confirmed a contract with Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd. and Mitsui Bussan Aerospace Co., Ltd. for obtaining optical imagery data essential for the project. Tri-Sat Constellation Co., Ltd., an SPC, is a key entity in this collaboration. This strategic move underlines Axelspace's commitment to advancing in the realm of satellite technology.

data.title: JPMorgan disputes Trump's $5 billion lawsuit, claims CEO Dimon wrongly sued. Lawsuit to shift to federal court due to legal reasons and lack of factual basis for allegations.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is seeking to shift President Donald Trump's lawsuit from Florida state court to federal court, arguing that CEO Jamie Dimon was wrongly included and that the state's Deceptive and Unfair Trade Practices Act does not apply to federally regulated bank executives. The legal team stated that since JPMorgan is an Ohio citizen and none of the plaintiffs are Ohio citizens, the case is eligible for federal court. They claimed Dimon was fraudulently named in the lawsuit to keep it in state court, arguing that Florida law exempts federally regulated bank executives. The bank refuted Trump's claims of being dropped as a client for political reasons, stating the allegations lack sufficient facts. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
